description: Mechanical verification registry for documented cpersona issues. Source of truth for bug-existence proof; checked by scripts/verify-issues.sh (mirrors the ClotoCore infrastructure).

issues.summary: (Bug A) Embedding env vars are read with the CPERSONA_ prefix only, but the marketplace catalog declares the generic EMBEDDING_MODE / EMBEDDING_HTTP_URL keys; an installed cpersona therefore runs with embeddings silently off and recall degrades to FTS-only
issues.fix_note: config.py now reads the generic EMBEDDING_MODE / EMBEDDING_HTTP_URL keys with a CPERSONA_* override (specific > generic, matching the CScheduler convention and the marketplace catalog), so a catalog-installed cpersona enables embeddings instead of silently running FTS-only. Pending version bump.

issues.summary: (Bug B) FTS keyword search builds the MATCH expression with query.split(); space-less scripts (Japanese/Chinese) collapse into a single phrase the trigram index never matches, so the keyword retriever is dead for Japanese and RRF loses its discriminating channel (recall-contamination precondition). Fix must pair a trigram query builder with a bm25-magnitude-aware merge (see ClotoCore/docs/RECALL_CONTAMINATION_AB_2026-06-14.md sec 10).
issues.fix_note: Replaced query.split() with _build_fts_query() (CJK trigram-OR decomposition) so the trigram FTS index is exercised for space-less scripts. Paired with the RSF merge (RECALL_MODE=rsf) which keeps the keyword channel's bm25 magnitude. Pending version bump.

issues.summary: (CRITICAL) HTTP Bearer auth bypass: BearerTokenMiddleware only rejects a request when the Authorization header is present-but-wrong; the `elif auth_token and not header: pass` branch lets header-less requests through to the app. With auth_token set and the default 0.0.0.0:8402 bind, any client on the network can call destructive tools (delete_agent_data, import_memories) with no token. Predates the package restructure (present in bf59f03^) and HTTP transport is entirely untested.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: BearerTokenMiddleware now rejects any missing/blank/malformed token with 401 (the header-less bypass branch is gone) and compares tokens with hmac.compare_digest. Regression: test_v2435_bugfixes.py auth tests + test_server_middleware_source_has_no_bypass_branch.

issues.summary: (HIGH) Schema migration swallows every ALTER failure with a bare `except Exception: pass` and then unconditionally stamps the latest SCHEMA_VERSION at the end. A transient failure (database is locked / disk I/O) leaves the column missing but the version marked current, so the migration is never retried and dependent writes (e.g. channel column) break permanently.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: migrations use idempotent _ensure_column existence checks (no swallowed ALTER errors) and the version stamp is withheld unless the ladder completes (guarded by `migration_error is None`), so a transient failure retries next boot. Regression: test_schema_v9_migration.py.

issues.summary: (HIGH) Task-queue poison-pill: _fetch_next runs json.loads(payload_json) outside the _loop try block. A single malformed payload row raises in the background task (started via asyncio.create_task with no done-callback), which dies silently; the row is not removed, so after a process restart the ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1 head re-crashes and the queue is permanently stuck with no log output.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: _fetch_next catches JSONDecodeError, discards the poison row and advances; start() attaches a done-callback surfacing abnormal loop exits; the drain body is wrapped so a transient fault re-arms instead of killing the loop. Pattern is the fix marker (additive fix). Regression: test_v2435_bugfixes.py poison-pill tests.

issues.summary: (HIGH) Queue-path archive_episode is a silent no-op that loses data. server.py enqueues an archive task only when summary is empty, but the dequeue side do_archive_episode returns immediately when summary is empty (returns {ok:True, episode_id:None}) without writing the episode. The task is deleted as completed and the caller gets {ok:true, queued:true}, so the loss is unobservable. Dead path left over after the server-side LLM summariser was removed.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: do_archive_episode_or_queue no longer enqueues an empty-summary archive (which the worker completed as a no-op, dropping the episode); it returns an explicit ok:false error. do_archive_episode's empty-summary path likewise returns ok:false. Regression: test_task_queue.py::test_tool_archive_episode_without_summary_is_rejected_not_queued.

issues.summary: (HIGH) check_health(fix=true) duplicate_content repair runs a global DELETE that ignores agent scope and locked rows. The detection query is agent-scoped, but the DELETE (id NOT IN SELECT MIN(id) GROUP BY agent_id, content) spans all agents and can delete locked memories, violating the lock_memory contract. Monthly maintenance for one agent mutates other agents' data.
issues.pattern: DELETE FROM memories WHERE id NOT IN \(SELECT MIN\(id\) FROM memories GROUP BY agent_id, content\)
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: the duplicate_content repair DELETE is now agent-scoped and filters `locked = 0`, so it never touches another agent's rows or a lock-protected memory. Regression: test_v2435_bugfixes.py check_health dedup tests.

issues.summary: (HIGH) memories_fts desync: the memories FTS5 external-content table has AFTER INSERT/DELETE triggers but no AFTER UPDATE trigger (episodes_fts has all three). do_update_memory compensates with a manual UPDATE of memories_fts run after the base content is already rewritten, which leaves stale trigrams in the index (and swallows errors with except: pass). check_health's desync check only compares COUNTs, so this row-count-preserving contamination is undetectable and old wording keeps matching new content (recall contamination).
issues.pattern: UPDATE memories_fts SET content = \? WHERE rowid = \?
issues.fix_note: v2.4.35: memories_fts gains an AFTER UPDATE trigger (SCHEMA_VERSION 10->11, with a one-time index rebuild) so in-place content edits stay in sync; the stale manual FTS UPDATE in do_update_memory is removed. Regression: test_v2435_bugfixes.py::test_update_memory_keeps_fts_in_sync.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) empty_channel fix conflicts with the ''=global channel semantics. maintenance_handlers rewrites channel '' to 'chat', but the recall path and migrate_channel_axis(globalize_unrecoverable=true) treat channel='' as global (matches all channel-scoped recalls). Running check_health(fix=true) after a globalize re-orphans those rows from non-chat channels. Leftover rule from the v6 migration worldview.
issues.fix_note: Fixed in v2.4.36: the empty_channel check/fix was removed from check_health; channel='' is the valid global channel under the ''=global semantics (recall matching + migrate_channel_axis globalize target).

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_store maps the new memory id via 'SELECT id ... ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1' after commit instead of cursor.lastrowid. Under concurrent stores (HTTP mode / queue running alongside) an interleaved INSERT makes it bind the wrong mem_id to the remote vector index, so recall returns a different memory's content (persistent contamination). The dedup check -> INSERT is also non-atomic (TOCTOU) with no UNIQUE constraint, allowing duplicate rows.
issues.pattern: SELECT id FROM memories WHERE agent_id = \? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1
issues.fix_note: Fixed in v2.4.36: do_store uses INSERT OR IGNORE + cursor.lastrowid for the remote index binding; SCHEMA_VERSION 11->12 adds UNIQUE dedup indexes (agent_id, project_id, channel, content) and a partial (agent_id, project_id, msg_id) WHERE msg_id != '' that make the dedup atomic. Migration collapses unlocked exact duplicates first (keep-MIN(id), never-touch-locked) and skips index creation non-fatally if a locked duplicate blocks it.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_update_memory rewrites content but never recomputes the embedding (and does not NULL it), so the stored vector stays for the old text and the row is not caught by check_health's null/dim-mismatch repair. The remote /index is also not updated. An edited rule memory keeps being recalled by its stale embedding.
issues.pattern: UPDATE memories SET content = \? WHERE id = \?
issues.fix_note: Fixed in v2.4.36: do_update_memory re-embeds the new content with the same policy as do_store (NULL on failure or when no client is configured, so check_health null-embedding repair applies) and re-sends the row to the remote /index.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The v11 FTS AFTER UPDATE triggers (memories_fts_au / episodes_au) fire on every column update, so do_recall's per-hit recall_count/last_recalled_at bump rewrites each hit's full trigram posting on every recall: ~20x slower hot-path UPDATE (p50 1.1ms vs 0.05ms, p95 8ms vs 0.07ms on a 2000-row corpus) and unbounded FTS index bloat (~29KB of dead postings per recall, +66% db size over 200 recalls). The maintenance embedding-only UPDATEs on episodes churn the same way.
issues.fix_note: Fixed after v2.4.36 (schema v13): both AU triggers are column-scoped (AFTER UPDATE OF content / OF summary, keywords) and guarded by WHEN old <> new, so recall-count bumps and embedding-only updates no longer touch the FTS index while real content edits still reindex (bug-008 guarantee preserved). Migration drops and recreates the triggers; the index itself is untouched so no rebuild is needed.

issues.summary: (HIGH) _autocut applies Weaviate-style gap detection to rank-fusion scores (_rrf_score/_rsf_score). RRF scores decay hyperbolically by construction, so the boundary between dual-retriever hits (~2/61) and single-retriever hits (~1/61) always shows a ~50% relative gap; on homogeneous corpora with little retriever overlap autocut slices a full recall to 2 rows regardless of the caller's limit (LMEB MemBench multi_session_assistant NDCG@10 = 0.0; +45.2 NDCG recovered with autocut off). The cut discards relevant memories far beyond the intended low-confidence trim.
issues.fix_note: Fixed after v2.4.36: _autocut keys on similarity-scale signals only (confidence when CONFIDENCE_ENABLED sorted the results, cosine for cascade ordering) and returns fusion-ordered results whole — RRF/RSF contamination control is the fused quality gate's job. Cascade and confidence gap-cut behaviour is unchanged (pinned by test_autocut_signal.py).

issues.summary: (HIGH) check_health(fix=true) duplicate_content repair groups only by (agent_id, content) at checks.py:109/121, ignoring the project_id/channel axes of the idx_memories_dedup_content UNIQUE contract; two legitimately distinct memories that share content across different project_id/channel gamma-buckets are collapsed into one duplicate group and every row except the global MIN(id) is DELETEd, silently and permanently destroying valid cross-bucket rows (e.g. the same text under project_id='' and 'cycia-mc') on the recommended monthly maintenance path.
issues.fix_sketch: GROUP BY agent_id, project_id, channel, content in BOTH the detection query and the survivor MIN(id) subquery to match idx_memories_dedup_content and the v12 migration.
issues.note: Initial audit framing (full-key collapse) was imprecise: the broad grouping's cross-CHANNEL cleanup is intentional (test _insert_dup). The real defect was crossing the PROJECT isolation axis; fix adds project_id (not channel) to the grouping.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: check_duplicate_content groups by (agent_id, project_id, content) in both detection and the MIN(id) survivor subquery — deliberately including project_id (a hard γ-isolation axis: the same content under different projects is legitimately distinct with different visibility) while still omitting channel (preserving the intended cross-channel cleanup the UNIQUE index leaves to check_health). Cross-project content is no longer collapsed, so the MIN(id) survivor can no longer delete the global copy and narrow another project's visibility. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_duplicate_content_does_not_collapse_across_projects; cross-channel dedup pinned by test_v2435_bugfixes.py.

issues.summary: (HIGH) deep_check(fix=true) deep_short_content selects rows with LENGTH(TRIM(content))<=5 and DELETEs them at checks.py:874 with no locked=0 guard on either the selection (:867) or the DELETE, violating the module docstring's stated bug-007 invariant that fixes never touch locked rows; a short memory the user explicitly locked (e.g. content='ACLs') is irreversibly deleted during routine maintenance.
issues.pattern: DELETE FROM memories WHERE id IN \(\{placeholders\}\) AND locked = 0
issues.fix_sketch: Add `AND locked = 0` to the deep_short_content selection and exclude locked ids from the DELETE.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: deep_short_content's DELETE now excludes locked rows (AND locked = 0) and reports cur.rowcount, so a user-locked short memory survives deep_check(fix=true). Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_locked_rows_survive_destructive_maintenance.

issues.summary: (HIGH) The export/import backup-restore path loses scope and crashes: do_export_memories never selects project_id/channel (admin_handlers.py:1115) and do_import_memories uses a plain INSERT that omits project_id/channel/embedding (:1252), so every restored row collapses into project_id=''/channel=''/NULL-embedding (gamma+channel isolation destroyed, rows invisible to vector recall); and because msg_id-less duplicate content violates the v12 idx_memories_dedup_content UNIQUE(agent_id,project_id,channel,content), the plain INSERT raises an uncaught IntegrityError (only json.JSONDecodeError is caught) that aborts the entire restore, while prior rows sit in the un-rolled-back shared transaction to be flushed by the next handler's commit.
issues.pattern: INSERT INTO memories \(agent_id, msg_id, content, source, timestamp, metadata\)
issues.fix_sketch: Use INSERT OR IGNORE, carry project_id/channel/embedding through export+import, and wrap the import loop in an explicit transaction with rollback on error.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: export now carries project_id/channel/locked (+ embedding via include_embeddings); import restores those columns, decodes the embedding, uses INSERT OR IGNORE (a dedup collision is a counted skip, not an IntegrityError abort), and runs the whole restore in one transaction that rolls back on error. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_export_import_roundtrip_preserves_axes_and_embedding, ::test_import_content_collision_skips_not_aborts.

issues.summary: (HIGH) The streamable-http transport fails open: auth_token defaults to '' (server.py:897) and BearerTokenMiddleware only enforces credentials inside `if auth_token:` (server.py:921), so an unset/empty CPERSONA_AUTH_TOKEN turns the middleware into a no-op while the server binds the default 0.0.0.0:8402 -- any network host can invoke all 24 tools unauthenticated, including delete_agent_data/delete_memory and export_memories/import_memories (caller-controlled file write/read), with no warning and no refusal to start. Distinct from the bug-003 header-less bypass, which only covered the token-set case.
issues.fix_sketch: Fail closed: refuse to start the HTTP transport when auth_token is empty, default CPERSONA_HTTP_HOST to 127.0.0.1, and log loudly whenever auth is disabled.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: HTTP transport fails closed — CPERSONA_HTTP_HOST defaults to 127.0.0.1 and _assert_safe_http_bind refuses an unauthenticated non-loopback bind (SystemExit); a loopback bind without a token warns loudly. The BearerTokenMiddleware `if auth_token:` gate is unchanged (still correct when a token is set); the exposure was the empty-token + public-bind startup, now guarded. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py HTTP-bind tests.

issues.summary: (HIGH) _autocut (memory_handlers.py:452-453) falls to key='_cosine' when the top row lacks _confidence/_rsf/_rrf scores and computes scores=[r.get(key) or 0 ...], so in cascade recall with CONFIDENCE_ENABLED=false (shipped default, the documented topic-drift workaround config) the stage-ordered vector-hit-first result list makes every non-vector (episode/keyword/profile) row score 0; this manufactures a full-scale gap at the vector->non-vector boundary and autocut truncates ALL non-vector hits whenever >=1 vector hit exists, silently collapsing multi-strategy cascade recall to vector-only. Same class as fixed bug-013 but for the cosine branch the rrf/rsf early-return does not cover.
issues.pattern: if any\(r\.get\("_cosine"\) is None for r in results\)
issues.fix_sketch: When the ordering signal is not uniformly present (rows lacking _cosine under cascade/confidence-off), skip autocut or run gap detection only over rows that carry the chosen score, mirroring the rrf/rsf early-return.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: _autocut's cosine fallback branch now returns the list whole when any row lacks _cosine (cascade concatenates non-vector stages that carry no cosine), instead of scoring the gap as 0 and truncating every non-vector hit. Homogeneous cosine-scored and confidence-sorted cuts are unchanged. Regression: test_autocut_signal.py::test_cascade_mixed_signal_is_not_cut.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) auto_tool's _handler (_vendored_mcp_common/mcp_utils.py:127) gates on `if default is not None`, so a param spec with an explicit None default -- ('project_id', str, None), used by recall/recall_with_context/list_memories/list_episodes to mean 'omit -> no filter (all projects)' -- is indistinguishable from having no default and validate_str substitutes ''; an omitted project_id therefore reaches the handler as '' (gamma_clause -> `project_id = ''` = global pool only), making the documented all-projects branch unreachable via MCP and silently excluding every project-bucketed (e.g. cycia-mc) row from session-start recall.
issues.fix_sketch: Track whether a default was supplied by spec arity (len(spec)>2) and forward an explicit None to the validator, so a None default reaches the handler as None instead of collapsing to ''.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_merge_memories (admin_handlers.py:1382) copies rows with a plain INSERT (no OR IGNORE) that omits project_id and only pre-skips rows with a non-empty msg_id; msg_id-less rows (the common do_store case) bypass dedup and collide with idx_memories_dedup_content whenever the target already holds identical (agent_id,'',channel,content), raising an uncaught IntegrityError that aborts the merge mid-loop -- defeating the documented strategy='skip' semantics -- and leaving prior INSERTs in the un-rolled-back shared transaction to be flushed by another handler's commit.
issues.pattern: INSERT INTO memories \(agent_id, msg_id, content, source, timestamp, metadata, channel\)
issues.fix_sketch: Use INSERT OR IGNORE (count skips via rowcount), preserve project_id, and wrap the merge loop in an explicit transaction with rollback on error.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: do_merge_memories copies rows with INSERT OR IGNORE (content collision -> counted skip, honouring strategy='skip'), preserves project_id, and wraps the merge in a transaction with rollback on error. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_merge_preserves_project_locked_and_skips_collision.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_migrate_channel_axis (maintenance_handlers.py:208-213 and :219) rewrites the channel column with two bare corpus-wide UPDATEs into the idx_memories_dedup_content UNIQUE index with no OR IGNORE and no try/except; if any recovered/globalized (agent_id,project_id,channel,content) equals an existing row, SQLite's default ABORT rolls back the whole statement (migrated=0), and because the collision is data-deterministic every re-run re-collides, so the knob2-flip channel migration can never complete on such a corpus.
issues.fix_sketch: Use UPDATE OR IGNORE (or a per-row try/except loop) so a single content collision skips that row and is reported, instead of aborting the whole migration.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_merge_memories source SELECT (admin_handlers.py:1366) omits the locked column and its INSERT never sets it, so every merged memory lands with locked=0; a memory the user explicitly locked loses its delete/edit protection after merge, and with mode='move' the original locked row is then removed by do_delete_agent_data's scope-wide DELETE, silently destroying the lock guarantee entirely.
issues.pattern: SELECT msg_id, content, source, timestamp, metadata, channel FROM memories WHERE agent_id = \?
issues.fix_sketch: Select `locked` in the source query and carry it through the INSERT column list so locked memories stay locked after merge.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: the merge source SELECT now reads project_id/channel/embedding/locked and the INSERT sets them, so merged memories keep their locked flag and γ-axes instead of landing unlocked in the global bucket. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_merge_preserves_project_locked_and_skips_collision.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_delete_memory deletes the DB row by id but computes the remote-vector removal namespace as `cpersona:{agent_id}` with agent_id defaulting to '' on the admin/dashboard path (agent_id not in the tool's required list), so admin_handlers.py:183 targets the empty namespace 'cpersona:' instead of the owner's `cpersona:{owner}` where do_store indexed it; under VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote the /remove is a no-op, the vector entry mem:{id} is orphaned permanently, and later recall wastes top-K slots on ghost hits that fail DB fetch, degrading recall. do_update_memory selects the owner and does this correctly.
issues.fix_sketch: SELECT the owner agent_id alongside locked and remove from cpersona:{owner} (mirror do_update_memory's row[1] handling) instead of the caller-supplied/empty agent_id.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: do_delete_memory reads the row's owner agent_id and builds the remote-vector namespace as cpersona:{owner_agent_id}, so a delete-by-id (no agent_id passed) removes the vector entry from the correct namespace instead of leaving it orphaned under 'cpersona:'.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_delete_memory (admin_handlers.py:173) and do_update_memory check the lock with a separate SELECT then issue an unguarded `DELETE/UPDATE ... WHERE id=? AND agent_id=?` with no `AND locked=0` folded into the DML; the statements straddle an await on the single shared aiosqlite connection, so over the concurrent HTTP transport a lock_memory committing locked=1 in the window is defeated and the now-locked memory is destroyed -- the same atomicity gap bug-007 fixed for the dedup DELETE, left uncovered in the admin path.
issues.pattern: WHERE id = \? AND agent_id = \? AND locked = 0
issues.fix_sketch: Fold `AND locked=0` into the DELETE/UPDATE DML and branch on cursor.rowcount so the lock check and mutation are one atomic statement.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_calibrate_threshold (admin_handlers.py:724) builds `vecs = np.array(vecs)` from raw np.frombuffer rows with no per-row dimension filter (same ragged construction in _temporal_adjacency_sims:431) and then does `vecs @ vecs.T`; on a mixed-embedding-dimension corpus (the jina-768d -> bge-1024d swap state the dim-change branch exists to handle) np.array of ragged vectors raises ValueError on numpy>=1.24, surfacing calibrate_threshold/set_recall_precision as an opaque {error} and, at ensure_calibrated_on_startup which is awaited before the transport try-block, able to abort server startup. The recall hot path already guards blob length; calibration does not.
issues.fix_sketch: Filter sampled rows to the modal/expected dimension (or detect length mismatch) and return ok:false with a clear 'mixed embedding dimensions' error instead of letting numpy raise.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) Every FTS backfill/rebuild migration step is gated `current < N and FTS_ENABLED` (database.py:254 etc.), so a DB first created with CPERSONA_FTS_ENABLED=false is stamped schema_version=13 with the non-FTS steps done; when FTS is later enabled, boot only runs the CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS FTS_SQL (empty virtual tables + triggers) and every version-gated rebuild is skipped, so all pre-existing memories/episodes are permanently absent from the FTS index and the keyword retriever silently returns zero hits for historical rows, dropping out of RRF/RSF/cascade with no error. Recoverable only via a manual check_health(fix=true) rebuild.
issues.fix_sketch: Track FTS-index-populated state separately from schema_version (or detect an empty *_fts over populated content) and run the ('rebuild')/backfill whenever FTS is enabled but the index is empty, regardless of current version.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The remote branch of _search_vector (vector.py:147) hardcodes `"min_similarity": _get_vector_threshold(agent_id)` and ignores the min_similarity argument the local branch honours; _recall_rrf/_recall_rsf deliberately pass a lowered threshold (0.5x) so fusion has more candidates to rank, so under VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote (default recall mode rrf) that lowered threshold is discarded and the endpoint over-filters at the full threshold, returning a strictly smaller, differently-ranked candidate set than local for identical data.
issues.fix_sketch: Send `min_similarity if min_similarity is not None else _get_vector_threshold(agent_id)` in the remote POST, matching the local branch's effective_min_sim semantics.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The content-rewriting fixers check_memory_annotation (checks.py:86), check_discord_mention (:100) and check_oversized_content (:139) mutate memory content on fix=true but never re-embed the row; FTS stays consistent via the memories_fts_au trigger, but the vector embedding still encodes the OLD content and no fixer repairs it (check_null_embedding only touches NULL blobs, check_embedding_dimension only NULLs wrong-length blobs), so vector recall scores the row on stale semantics indefinitely -- the bug-011 failure class re-introduced in checks.py.
issues.pattern: UPDATE memories SET content = \? WHERE id = \?
issues.fix_sketch: After each content mutation, re-embed via vector._embedding_client + /index (as do_update_memory does post-bug-011), or set embedding=NULL so check_null_embedding re-embeds it.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) check_oversized_content truncates content via `UPDATE memories SET content = SUBSTR(content,1,?) WHERE id=?` (checks.py:139) with no locked=0 guard on the selection (:131) or the UPDATE, violating the documented bug-007 locked-row invariant; a memory longer than MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH that the user locked (reachable because import_memories inserts content un-sanitized) is irreversibly truncated during routine check_health(fix=true), also leaving a stale embedding.
issues.pattern: SUBSTR\(content, 1, \?\), embedding = NULL WHERE id = \? AND locked = 0
issues.fix_sketch: Add `AND locked = 0` to the oversized selection and UPDATE (and re-embed after truncation).
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: check_oversized_content's truncate UPDATE excludes locked rows (AND locked = 0), so a locked oversized memory keeps its full content. Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_locked_rows_survive_destructive_maintenance.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) check_empty_content's fix DELETEs whitespace-only rows agent-scoped but without the `locked = 0` guard (checks.py:693) that its sibling check_duplicate_content applies and the module docstring promises; a locked memory whose content is whitespace-only (reachable via import_memories/merge_memories which insert content un-stripped) is destroyed by check_health(fix=true), breaking the bug-007 invariant.
issues.pattern: \(TRIM\(content\) = '' OR content IS NULL\) AND locked = 0
issues.fix_sketch: Add `AND locked = 0` to the empty_content DELETE.
issues.fix_note: v2.4.38: check_empty_content's DELETE excludes locked rows (AND locked = 0). Regression: test_v2438_hardening.py::test_locked_rows_survive_destructive_maintenance.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) check_stale_pending_tasks counts (checks.py:657) and DELETEs (:664) rows in pending_memory_tasks with only `WHERE created_at < datetime('now','-1 hour')` and no agent_id predicate, though the table is per-agent and every sibling check applies _agent_scope; running check_health(agent_id='A', fix=true) deletes EVERY agent's >1h-old un-drained memory-write tasks, so another agent's stuck store is silently discarded and never persisted -- cross-agent data loss, same class as bug-007's scope leak in a distinct location.
issues.fix_sketch: Apply the _agent_scope clause to both the count and the DELETE so only the target agent's stale tasks are removed.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) do_recall/do_recall_with_context take limit straight from the tool call and never clamp it (memory_handlers.py:803 `results = results[:limit]`, plus raw `LIMIT ?` in the FTS/no-query keyword paths), while _clamp_limit IS applied in do_list_memories/do_list_episodes; a negative limit reaches SQLite as `LIMIT -1` (unbounded), so recall materializes and scores the agent's entire corpus on the hot path and the trailing results[:-1] then silently drops rows -- an unbounded-scan/resource-exhaustion vector for a non-conforming client.
issues.fix_sketch: Call _clamp_limit(limit, CAP) at the top of do_recall/do_recall_with_context (mirroring the list handlers) before limit reaches SQL LIMIT or the slice.

issues.summary: (MEDIUM) The remote /search POST (vector.py:141) uses `_embedding_client._client.post` with no per-call timeout, inheriting the client's 30s DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECS on the recall hot path; the module defines PROBE_TIMEOUT_SECS=3.0 precisely so the probe does NOT inherit the 30s, but the actual recall query does -- so under VECTOR_SEARCH_MODE=remote a hung/flapping endpoint blocks every recall ~30s before the except fires and falls back to local, collapsing throughput.
issues.fix_sketch: Pass an explicit short timeout (e.g. PROBE_TIMEOUT_SECS or a dedicated remote-search budget) to the /search POST so a hung endpoint falls back to local quickly.

issues.summary: (LOW) The FTS-miss keyword fallback builds the LIKE pattern as f"%{query}%" (memory_handlers.py:1078) against `AND content LIKE ?` with no ESCAPE clause, so % and _ in the user query act as wildcards rather than literals (the adjacent source_id filter IS correctly escaped with _like_escape_prefix + ESCAPE); a query like 'a_b' or '50%' silently over-matches unrelated rows, polluting the keyword channel that feeds RRF fusion. Parameterized, so no SQL injection.
issues.fix_sketch: Escape the query with the source_id helper (escape backslash, %, _) and add ESCAPE to the content LIKE clause, mirroring the source_id path.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_recall_with_context filters/uses caller-supplied external_context via `e.get("content", "").strip()` (memory_handlers.py:872, and again at :888); the '' default only applies when the key is absent, so an entry with an explicit content=null (JSON null -> None) makes None.strip() raise AttributeError, aborting the whole recall_with_context call into an opaque {error} instead of skipping the one malformed entry (the tool schema declares no type constraint on content).
issues.fix_sketch: Coerce defensively to str: `(e.get('content') or '').strip()` at both call sites (:872 and :888).

issues.summary: (LOW) do_delete_agent_data (admin_handlers.py, DELETE at :305) wipes the agent's memories/profiles/episodes and remote vector namespace but never removes the agent's entries from vector._agent_thresholds/_agent_fused_gates/_agent_betas nor rewrites the calibration sidecar, so the stale per-agent threshold/beta survive in-process and are re-loaded on restart via _restore_calibration_state; a later same-id agent silently inherits calibration computed from the deleted corpus and under-/over-recalls until recalibration.
issues.fix_sketch: After the purge, pop agent_id from vector._agent_thresholds/_agent_fused_gates/_agent_betas and re-persist the calibration sidecar so no stale per-agent state survives the wipe.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_migrate_channel_axis relies on the invariant 'whatever is still discord is exactly the unrecoverable bucket' but executes the globalize `UPDATE memories SET channel='' WHERE channel='discord'` (maintenance_handlers.py:219) after the recoverable UPDATE with an await boundary between them on the shared connection; over the concurrent HTTP transport a do_store landing a new snowflake-session 'discord' row in that window is swept to channel='' instead of its concrete channel -- a silent channel-scope broadening (global-visibility) leak, not data loss.
issues.pattern: UPDATE memories SET channel = '' WHERE channel = 'discord'
issues.fix_sketch: Restrict the globalize UPDATE to genuinely-unrecoverable rows (WHERE channel='discord' AND NOT (session_id GLOB snowflake)) so a concurrently-inserted recoverable row is not swept into global.

issues.summary: (LOW) do_recall bumps recall_count/last_recalled_at and commits (memory_handlers.py:843) for every returned memory; the tool is registered readOnlyHint=true and is deliberately NOT one of the no_persist-gated write tools, yet those columns feed _compute_confidence's ranking, so a session put into no_persist mode for benchmarking/AB still permanently mutates confidence-ranking state (when CONFIDENCE_ENABLED) on every recall -- the exact contamination no_persist exists to prevent. Gated behind the default-off CONFIDENCE_ENABLED flag.
issues.pattern: UPDATE memories SET recall_count = recall_count \+ 1, last_recalled_at = datetime\('now'\)
issues.fix_sketch: Skip the recall_count/last_recalled_at bump when no_persist.is_paused() (and reconsider readOnlyHint) so a no-persist session leaves ranking state unmutated.

issues.summary: (LOW) Every gamma-semantics assertion in test_isolation.py invokes the handler coroutines directly with project_id=None (e.g. :172), never through the ToolRegistry.auto_tool -> validate_str extraction the live MCP call uses, so the suite proves the handler's gamma math but gives false confidence that 'omit project_id -> all projects' works end-to-end; the omission->global-only wiring defect (bug-019) passes CI green because no test drives the registered tool handler with project_id absent.
issues.fix_sketch: Add a test that calls the registered tool handler (registry._handlers['recall']/'list_memories') with an arguments dict omitting project_id and asserts all-bucket rows are returned.
